methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Liminal Insights is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Barry Van Tassell and Shaurjo Biswas that has raised $38.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), all operating in the same sector and stage cohort.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $39M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $78M. Peer-median analysis references three same-stage climate and energy companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), producing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M–$200M. The peer data reflects observable market pricing for comparable-stage entrants in this sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $66M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $114M. The 160-basis-point spread between methods reflects legitimate tension between recent capital efficiency (stage-progression) and peer-set positioning (peer-median). Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three private peers, recent market conditions in climate-tech fundraising, and whether Liminal's specific technical or commercial profile merits a premium or discount within the peer range. Further diligence on unit economics and competitive positioning would help narrow the indicated range.
